question_text: Name one difference between a permanent magnet and an electromagnet.
correct_answer: An electromagnet only works when electric current flows through it, whereas a permanent magnet is always magnetic. (Accept: An electromagnet can be switched on and off, or a permanent magnet does not need electricity.)
explanation: Electromagnets are temporary magnets that depend on electricity to create their magnetic field, so they can be turned on and off. Permanent magnets always produce a magnetic field without needing any power source.
